title: सूर्य सहस्रनाम ध्यानम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री सूर्य सहस्रनाम ध्यानम् ॥

ध्येयः सदा सवितृमण्डलमध्यवर्ती
नारायणः सरसिजासनसन्निविष्टः।
केयूरवान् मकरकुण्डलवान् किरीटी
हारी हिरण्मयवपुर्धृतशङ्खचक्रः॥ १॥

आदित्यहृदयं तस्मात् सर्वशत्रुविनाशनम्।
जयावहं जपेन्नित्यमक्षय्यं परमं शुभम्॥ २॥

उद्यन्नद्यविवस्वानारोहन्नुत्तरां दिवम्।
देवः सहस्ररश्मिः शतधा वर्तमानः॥ ३॥

तच्चक्षुर्देवहितं पुरस्ताच्छुक्रमुच्चरत्।
पश्येम शरदः शतं जीवेम शरदः शतम्॥ ४॥

सवितुर्वरेण्यं भर्गो देवस्य धीमहि।
धियो यो नः प्रचोदयात्॥ ५॥

जपाकुसुमसंकाशं काश्यपेयं महाद्युतिम्।
तमोऽरिं सर्वपापघ्नं प्रणतोऽस्मि दिवाकरम्॥ ६॥

सप्ताश्वरथमारूढं प्रचण्डं कश्यपात्मजम्।
श्वेतपद्मधरं देवं तं सूर्यं प्रणमाम्यहम्॥ ७॥

सूर्यं सुन्दरलोकनाथममृतं वेदान्तसारं शिवं
ज्ञानं ब्रह्ममयं सुरेशममलं लोकैकचित्तम् स्वयम्।
इन्द्रादित्यनराधिपं सुरगुरुं त्रैलोक्यचूडामणिं
ब्रह्मविष्णुशिवस्वरूपहृदयं वन्दे सदा भास्करम्॥ ८॥

भास्वान् भानुर्विवस्वान् भगवान्
तपनः सविता रविः।
सहस्रांशुः सहस्रार्चिः
सप्तसप्तिर्मरीचिमान्॥ ९॥

तिमिरोन्मथनः शम्भुस्त्वष्टा
मार्तण्ड अंशुमान्।
हिरण्यगर्भः शिशिरस्तपनो
भास्करो रविः॥ १०॥

॥ इति श्री सूर्य सहस्रनाम ध्यानम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Surya Sahasranama Dhyanam ||

Dhyeyah Sada Savitri Mandala Madhyavarti
Narayanah Sarasijasana Sannivishtah |
Keyuravan Makara Kundalavan Kiriti
Hari Hiranmaya Vapur Dhrita Shankha Chakrah || 1 ||

Aditya Hridayam Tasmat Sarva Shatru Vinashanam |
Jayavaham Japen Nityam Akshayam Paramam Shubham || 2 ||

Udyann Adya Vivasvaan Arohann Uttaram Divam |
Devah Sahasra Rashmih Satadha Vartamanah || 3 ||

Tat Chakshur Devahitam Purastat Chhukram Uchcharat |
Pashyema Sharadah Shatam Jivema Sharadah Shatam || 4 ||

Savitur Varenyam Bhargo Devasya Dhimahi |
Dhiyo Yo Nah Prachodayat || 5 ||

Japa Kusuma Sankasham Kashyapeyam Mahadyutim |
Tamo'rim Sarva Papaghnam Pranato'smi Divaakaram || 6 ||

Saptashva Ratham Arudham Prachandam Kashyapatmajam |
Shveta Padma Dharam Devam Tam Suryam Pranamamy Aham || 7 ||

Suryam Sundara Lokanatham Amritam Vedanta Saram Shivam
Jnanam Brahmamayam Suresham Amalam Lokaika Chittam Svayam |
Indraditya Naradhipam Suragurum Trailokya Chudamanim
Brahma Vishnu Shiva Svarupa Hridayam Vande Sada Bhaskaram || 8 ||

Bhasvan Bhanur Vivasvan Bhagavan
Tapanah Savita Ravih |
Sahasramshuh Sahasrarchih
Sapta Saptir Marichiman || 9 ||

Timironmathanah Shambhus Tvashta
Martanda Amshuman |
Hiranyagarbhah Shishiras Tapano
Bhaskaro Ravih || 10 ||

|| Iti Shri Surya Sahasranama Dhyanam Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
सदैव सवितृमण्डल (सूर्यमण्डल) के मध्य में विराजमान, कमलासन पर बैठे, केयूर-मकरकुण्डल-किरीट धारी, हार पहने, हिरण्यमय शरीर वाले, शंख-चक्रधारी नारायण का ध्यान करना चाहिए।

आदित्यहृदय सब शत्रुओं का नाश करने वाला, जय देने वाला, अक्षय और परम शुभ है।

उदय होते हुए, उत्तर दिशा में आकाश पर चढ़ते हुए, सहस्र किरणों वाले, सैकड़ों प्रकार से वर्तमान देव सूर्य।

गायत्री मन्त्र: उस सविता देवता के श्रेष्ठ तेज का हम ध्यान करें, जो हमारी बुद्धि को प्रेरित करे।

जपाकुसुम के समान लाल, कश्यप के पुत्र, महातेजस्वी, अन्धकार के शत्रु, सर्वपापनाशक दिवाकर को प्रणाम।

सात अश्वों के रथ पर आरूढ़, प्रचण्ड, कश्यपात्मज, श्वेत पद्मधारी सूर्यदेव को नमस्कार।

सुन्दर, लोकनाथ, अमृतस्वरूप, वेदान्तसार, शिव, ज्ञानस्वरूप, ब्रह्ममय, सुरेश, त्रिलोकी का चूड़ामणि, ब्रह्मा-विष्णु-शिव स्वरूप भास्कर को सदा वन्दन।
benefits: Powerful dhyana shlokas from Surya Sahasranama for sun worship
Combines Gayatri mantra with Surya meditation verses
Invokes health, vitality, eyesight and confidence
Traditionally recited for removal of all enemies and removes obstacles
Ideal for Sunday worship and Surya puja
description: The Surya Sahasranama Dhyanam, known in Sanskrit as सूर्य सहस्रनाम ध्यानम्, is a sacred recitation of 1000 divine names in praise of Sun God Surya. Attributed to Bhavishya Purana / Surya Sahasranama, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to destroy all enemies and remove obstacles as well as grant health, vitality, eyesight and confidence. Additionally, it is known to combine Gayatri mantra with Surya meditation verses. Recitation is particularly auspicious on Sunday (Ravivar) and during Makar Sankranti, Ratha Saptami, and Chhath Puja. At 10 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 5 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Surya Sahasranama Dhyanam in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Surya Sahasranama Dhyanam in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Meditation verses for Sun God worship with Gayatri mantra.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Surya Sahasranama Dhyanam?
seoFaqs.answer: The Surya Sahasranama Dhyanam is the meditation prelude (Dhyana Shlokas) recited before the full thousand-name (Sahasranama) recitation of the Sun God. It is not the complete Sahasranama itself but the introductory invocation that establishes the meditator's mental image of the deity, prepares the psyche for the recitation, and unites the practitioner with the form being worshipped. The Dhyana includes the celebrated 'Dhyeyah Sada Savitri Mandala', the Gayatri verse, the 'Japa Kusuma Sankasham' meditation, and excerpts of the Aditya Hridayam invocation.

seoFaqs.question: How is the Dhyanam different from the full Surya Sahasranama?
seoFaqs.answer: The Dhyanam is the short opening section (about 10 verses) that contains visualisation shlokas, the Gayatri Mantra, and key benedictory verses. The Surya Sahasranama proper is the much longer recitation of 1000 names of the Sun God arranged in approximately 100-120 verses, found in the Bhavishya Purana and the Brahma Purana. Devotees who do not have time for the full Sahasranama often recite only the Dhyanam — preserving the spirit of Surya meditation in a brief 5-minute practice while still receiving the benediction associated with sincere Surya worship.

seoFaqs.question: What is the source of the Surya Sahasranama Dhyanam?
seoFaqs.answer: The Dhyana shlokas are drawn from the Bhavishya Purana — the principal source of the full Surya Sahasranama — together with the Aditya Hridayam from the Yuddha Kanda of Valmiki's Ramayana, the Surya Upanishad, and the Rigveda Gayatri verse. The opening 'Dhyeyah Sada Savitri Mandala Madhyavarti Narayanah' is widely chanted as a daily Vishnu-Surya invocation. This compilation reflects the classical understanding of Surya as Surya-Narayana — the Sun as a visible form of Vishnu.

seoFaqs.question: What are the benefits of reciting the Surya Sahasranama Dhyanam?
seoFaqs.answer: Traditional sources associate the Dhyanam with the destruction of enemies (sarva-shatru-vinashana), the gift of victory (jayavaham), inexhaustible spiritual merit (akshayam), and the highest auspiciousness (paramam shubham). It is recited as a planetary remedy for an afflicted Sun, as a prayer for health and vitality, for sharp eyesight (since Surya rules the eyes in Ayurveda), for confidence and leadership, and as a preparatory meditation before the full Sahasranama. Daily recitation is held to bring radiance, clarity of intellect, and freedom from obstacles.

seoFaqs.question: When should the Surya Sahasranama Dhyanam be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The recommended time is sunrise (Brahma Muhurta or Pratah Sandhya), facing east, while offering Arghya to the rising Sun. Sunday (Ravivar) is the dedicated day, and recitation is especially auspicious on Ratha Saptami (the Sun's transit-birthday), Makar Sankranti (Uttarayana), and during Chhath Puja. Devotees aiming for graha-shanti (planetary peace) for a weak Sun in the horoscope often chant it daily for forty-three days (one mandala) or for six months as part of an extended Surya Sadhana.

seoFaqs.question: What deity is invoked in the Surya Sahasranama Dhyanam?
seoFaqs.answer: The Dhyanam invokes Surya in his integrated form as Surya-Narayana — the Sun visualised at the centre of the solar disc as a manifestation of Vishnu, seated on a lotus, wearing armlets (Keyura), crocodile-shaped earrings (Makara Kundala), a crown (Kiriti), and a garland (Hari), with a golden body (Hiranmaya Vapur) holding the conch and discus. He is also invoked as the seven-horse-chariot rider, son of Kashyapa, holder of the white lotus, and as Brahma-Vishnu-Shiva-svarupa — the unified form of the Trimurti.

seoFaqs.question: How many verses does the Surya Sahasranama Dhyanam contain?
seoFaqs.answer: The Dhyanam contains ten verses in this compilation. These include the central 'Dhyeyah Sada Savitri Mandala' visualisation, the 'Aditya Hridayam Tasmat' benedictory verse, two Vedic verses including the Gayatri Mantra, the 'Japa Kusuma Sankasham' colour-meditation, the seven-horse-chariot visualisation, the 'Suryam Sundara Lokanatham' integrated Trimurti invocation, and two verses listing the principal Vedic and Puranic names of the Sun (Bhasvan, Bhanu, Vivasvan, Tapana, Savita, Hiranyagarbha, Martanda, Bhaskara, etc.).

seoFaqs.question: Should the Dhyanam be recited before the full Sahasranama?
seoFaqs.answer: Yes, in the traditional ritual sequence the Dhyana shlokas are always recited first to establish the meditative state, fix the visualisation of the deity, and offer the Sankalpa (intention). The Dhyanam is followed by the 1000 names, then the Phalashruti (declaration of fruits), and finally the Aarti or Pranama. When time is limited, devotees may recite only the Dhyanam as a complete short practice, which is considered to carry the essence of the longer recitation through the principle of upalakshana (the part standing for the whole).

seoFaqs.question: Is the Surya Sahasranama Dhyanam in Sanskrit?
seoFaqs.answer: Yes, the Dhyanam is composed in classical Sanskrit using a mixture of Anushtubh and Shardula-vikridita metres. On this page the text is presented in Devanagari script with full English (IAST-style) transliteration and Hindi translation to support correct pronunciation and comprehension. The visualisation shlokas use rich descriptive vocabulary drawn from Puranic iconography, while the Vedic verses preserve the original Rigvedic and Yajurvedic phrasing of the Gayatri and Pashyema-Sharadah-Shatam mantras.
